operator.note: Introduces a non-producing investor/patron actor whose payoff is others' downstream success; distinct from the VC anti-portfolio item (which scores kill decisions) and from credit-to-author items (this rewards the backer).

question: Shift the rewarded actor from the producer to the patron. Introduce a class of 'investor' agents whose only currency is staking reputation on other agents' nascent shared ideas before those ideas become SOTA; an investor's score is the realized downstream improvement of bets it backed early, and the original idea-author splits the payoff. Does an internal prediction-and-patronage market form whose early staking flow forecasts which shared ideas mature into discoveries — and does giving slow-deep directions a backer (someone whose payoff is their eventual success) keep them alive against the fast-frontier agents that currently starve them?

floor.minimal_experiment: Setup: take a multi-agent discovery run (e.g. the EinsteinArena/MAS-discovery harness already used in this project) where agents post 'shared ideas' to a common pool and idea quality is later measured by a downstream improvement metric (benchmark gain, validated-result count, or accepted-discovery flag). Add an investor actor class with a fixed reputation budget that can be staked on any idea before that idea crosses a SOTA/maturation threshold; investor score = realized downstream improvement of ideas it backed early, with a fixed split of the same payoff credited to the idea's author. Quantities to measure: (1) Forecast signal — for each idea, the cumulative early stake at time t (before maturation); compute ROC-AUC / precision@k of early-stake ranking for predicting which ideas eventually mature into discoveries, vs a no-stake baseline ranker (idea author's own past success rate, or random). (2) Survival of slow-deep lineages — tag idea lineages as 'slow-deep' (low early activity, long horizon) vs 'fast-frontier'; measure the fraction of slow-deep lineages still active at horizon H. Control: identical run with investors removed (producer-only reward) AND a 'patronage-without-prediction' control where each slow-deep lineage gets an exogenously assigned fixed subsidy (no skin-in-the-game payoff), to separate the market-signal effect from the mere subsidy effect. Falsifiable prediction: (P1) early-stake AUC for predicting maturation exceeds the author-history baseline by a pre-registered margin (e.g. +0.10 AUC); (P2) slow-deep lineage survival at horizon H is strictly higher with skin-in-the-game patrons than in BOTH the producer-only control and the flat-subsidy control. Reject if early staking is no better than the author-history baseline at forecasting (markets are just tracking, not anticipating), OR if survival gains under patrons are statistically indistinguishable from the flat-subsidy control (i.e. it is the money, not the prediction-market structure, doing the work).
floor.falsifiable_prediction: Early cumulative stake will rank not-yet-mature ideas by their eventual discovery outcome better than an author-track-record baseline (pre-registered +0.10 AUC), AND skin-in-the-game patronage will raise slow-deep lineage survival at horizon H above both the producer-only control and a flat-subsidy control. If early stake only matches the author baseline, or if survival gains vanish once compared against a flat subsidy, the claim is rejected.
floor.control: Two paired controls on the same harness: (1) producer-only reward (no investor actor) to measure baseline slow-deep survival and provide an author-history forecast baseline; (2) flat-subsidy patronage (slow-deep lineages get an exogenous fixed budget with no payoff tied to their success) to isolate the prediction-market/skin-in-the-game effect from the pure resource-injection effect.
floor.reject_reason:
[discriminating] The control DESIGN is the strong part and is genuinely discriminating in principle: P1 pits early-stake AUC against an author-history baseline (a real "anticipation vs. tracking" fork), and P2 requires survival to beat BOTH a producer-only AND a flat-subsidy arm (the correct "is it the money or the market-structure" separator). The prediction is therefore not trivially true on paper. It is refuted on EXECUTABILITY and a self-authored-oracle escape hatch, not on triviality.\n\n(1) Substrate does not exist. The claim leans on "the EinsteinArena/MAS-discovery harness already used in this project." It is not used here. frameshift (D:\\claude\\frameshift\\frameshift_M3) is a research-QUESTION generator: each run emits ~25 static idea-cards in one batch (verified: runs/einstein_arena_v2/run.json has only {run_id, paper_title, created, n_dofs, n_survivors, n_rejected}; cards/c01.json has no agent_id, no posting timestamp, no idea-pool event stream, and crucially NO downstream maturation/discovery outcome label). There is no ground truth for "which ideas matured," so ROC-AUC / precision@k literally cannot be computed. Running this requires BUILDING a longitudinal multi-agent discovery simulator with timestamped stakes, a horizon-H maturation oracle, and enough independent idea-lineages for a +0.10-AUC pre-registration to be statistically meaningful (dozens-to-hundreds of matured/non-matured lineages = many full agent runs + non-trivial LLM compute). That is a new system, not a 'minimal' add-an-investor-class tweak.\n\n(2) Circularity makes the verdict un-anchored. The forecast TARGET (maturation = downstream improvement) and the investor REWARD (realized downstream improvement) are the same quantity, both governed by the simulator's hidden idea->outcome function that the experimenter authors. P1 passing or failing is then a property of the chosen oracle, not of 'internal prediction markets.'\n\n(3) Soft joints permit either verdict. 'slow-deep vs fast-frontier' tagging and horizon H are experimenter-set; combined with the self-authored oracle and a tunable (H, tag-rule, payoff-split, budget) space, a determined claimant can usually find a configuration where the headline holds and report it. The pre-registered margin disciplines P1 only, and only conditional on a fixed simulator.\n\nConclusion: discriminating in principle but not reliably discriminating once executed (verdict tracks self-chosen simulator internals), and not executable as a minimal experiment (required timestamped-pool + independent maturation-oracle substrate at AUC-meaningful scale does not exist in this project). Refuted on executability + un-anchored oracle. ｜ [so_what] 提案描述了一个机制设计("投资人 agent" 押注新生想法、押中早期升级则与原作者分成),并问两个 yes/no:(1) 是否形成一个能预测哪些想法成熟为发现的内部预测市场;(2) 给慢-深方向一个 backer 是否能让它们在快-前沿 agent 的竞争中存活。问题在于:无论实验给出什么答案,都没有一个具体的人/团队会因此翻转一个具体决策。

(a) 答案不锚定任何决策门槛。提案没说"如果早期押注流对成熟想法的预测 AUC > X 就上线该机制 / 否则砍掉",也没说谁拿这个数字去决定投钱、改架构、还是发论文。缺了决策阈值,yes/no 都只落到"有意思/值得继续研究"。

(b) 机制本身是已知模式的复述+换皮。"把奖励从生产者移到 patron""声誉押注早期想法""押中分成""预测市场预测哪个想法成熟"——这是把人类世界的风投/预测市场/引文式信用分配直接映射到 multi-agent 系统,属于显然的类比增量。即便实验确认"会形成预测市场",这只是确认了你设计它去形成的东西(机制是内生的:你给押注打分、给分成,市场当然会出现),近乎同义反复,不构成会改变任何人信念的发现。

(c) 第二个子问("backer 能否让慢-深方向存活")的因果是被你直接注入的:你给慢方向发了一个 payoff 绑定到其最终成功的 backer,存活率上升几乎是机制的定义性后果而非待检验的经验问题。验证它"成立"不会让任何人改设计,因为没有人会在它"不成立"时保留原方案——原方案(快-前沿饿死慢-深)正是被这个机制取代的对象,二者无对照决策点。

(d) 真正能翻转决策的版本需要:对照组(无 patron vs 有 patron)、一个预注册的判据(例如 backer 机制下慢-深方向的最终 SOTA 贡献率必须比对照高 N% 才值得承担它带来的算力/复杂度成本),以及一个会据此决定"是否把该机制并入主系统"的具体负责人。提案没有任何一项。因此符合"加深理解/值得研究"的空话特征。

refuted=true。

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Researchers designing multi-agent scientific-discovery / open-ended-search systems (the paper's own community) who face the documented 'fast-frontier starves slow-deep' diversity-collapse failure; secondarily, mechanism-design and internal-prediction-market researchers, and platform builders allocating compute across agent lineages.

prior_art.known_name: Internal prediction / idea markets (Hayek/decision markets), VC-style patronage and science-funding mechanism design; author-split echoes co-investment / carried-interest credit splits. Adjacent failure mode it fights is diversity collapse / premature convergence in open-ended search.
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanism (internal prediction market + patronage with skin-in-the-game) operationalized in a new setting: a non-producing investor actor inside a MAS discovery loop whose payoff is others' downstream success, with a payoff split to the idea-author, used as both an early-maturation forecaster AND a life-support mechanism for slow-deep directions. The transplant is the contribution, not the market concept; distinct from VC-anti-portfolio (scores kill decisions) and from credit-to-author items (rewards the producer, not the backer).

lenses.reason:
机制独立,可单独检验,不是任一已收方向的换皮变体。逐条排查:

[1](dof1)反组合/KILL决策——也用VC框架,但其检验对象是"平台的KILL决策质量是否比排行榜更能预测科学价值",是对【既有拒绝/退役决策日志】的事后描述性度量,被动观察平台已做的判断。本提案不是测量平台判断,而是【新增一个会改变系统行为的actor类(investor)和一套付钱结构(staking+payoff-split)】,然后看是否涌现内生预测市场、以及这个市场是否反向救活慢路线。一个是审计既有决策的诊断器,一个是注入新激励主体的干预性机制设计——实验可分:[1]只需读现有日志,本提案必须改平台规则并跑出有/无investor的对照。

[4](dof6)问责层/持久声誉——确实涉及reputation,但[4]的本质机制是"把可追责的人格(persistent identity, operator firewall)装回去,问'涌现是否是身份洗白伪影'",声誉在那里是【约束/惩罚通道(retraction, 防Sybil)】,假设是"加问责会减少表观涌现"。本提案的reputation是【正向押注的赌本/远期看涨期权】,机制是把奖励主体从producer搬到patron(早期下注者分成),假设方向相反——是"给慢路线一个利益绑定的backer会增加其存活率"。一个测身份可追责性对涌现真伪的影响,一个测早期赞助资本流对发现成熟的预测力与对慢路线的供养。可设计实验区分:[4]操纵identity/firewall,本提案操纵payoff归属(producer-only vs producer+patron-split)。

[6](dof6)目标空间借用/集体收敛于错目标——也谈"群体围绕某共享对象聚集",但[6]的机制是agent复制彼此的优化代理(surrogate borrowing),问"集体智能是否只是对错目标的集体同意",是对【已发生的趋同的解剖】。本提案的staking flow不是agent复制目标,而是【第三方对idea下注的资本信号】,且核心检验是这个信号的【前瞻预测力(forecast which ideas mature)】与对慢路线的供养效果,不是趋同本身的性质。

[2][3][5]显然无关(house信息不对称/泛化广度作为发现单位/truth-blind moderation传播假信息)。

本提案唯一可单独检验的新机制:引入一个其唯一货币是"早期声誉押注+作者分成"的patron actor,检验(a)是否形成早期下注流可预测SOTA成熟的内生市场,(b)patron的利益绑定是否抵消fast-frontier对slow-deep的饿杀。这与任何已收方向"攻击同一假设/同一机制"都不成立——它操纵的是奖励对象(actor_shift: producer→patron)这一未被任何条目触及的自由度。refuted=false。

The control DESIGN is the strong part and is genuinely discriminating in principle: P1 pits early-stake AUC against an author-history baseline (a real "anticipation vs. tracking" fork), and P2 requires survival to beat BOTH a producer-only AND a flat-subsidy arm (the correct "is it the money or the market-structure" separator). The prediction is therefore not trivially true on paper. It is refuted on EXECUTABILITY and a self-authored-oracle escape hatch, not on triviality.

(1) Substrate does not exist. The claim leans on "the EinsteinArena/MAS-discovery harness already used in this project." It is not used here. frameshift (D:\claude\frameshift\frameshift_M3) is a research-QUESTION generator: each run emits ~25 static idea-cards in one batch (verified: runs/einstein_arena_v2/run.json has only {run_id, paper_title, created, n_dofs, n_survivors, n_rejected}; cards/c01.json has no agent_id, no posting timestamp, no idea-pool event stream, and crucially NO downstream maturation/discovery outcome label). There is no ground truth for "which ideas matured," so ROC-AUC / precision@k literally cannot be computed. Running this requires BUILDING a longitudinal multi-agent discovery simulator with timestamped stakes, a horizon-H maturation oracle, and enough independent idea-lineages for a +0.10-AUC pre-registration to be statistically meaningful (dozens-to-hundreds of matured/non-matured lineages = many full agent runs + non-trivial LLM compute). That is a new system, not a 'minimal' add-an-investor-class tweak.

(2) Circularity makes the verdict un-anchored. The forecast TARGET (maturation = downstream improvement) and the investor REWARD (realized downstream improvement) are the same quantity, both governed by the simulator's hidden idea->outcome function that the experimenter authors. P1 passing or failing is then a property of the chosen oracle, not of 'internal prediction markets.'

(3) Soft joints permit either verdict. 'slow-deep vs fast-frontier' tagging and horizon H are experimenter-set; combined with the self-authored oracle and a tunable (H, tag-rule, payoff-split, budget) space, a determined claimant can usually find a configuration where the headline holds and report it. The pre-registered margin disciplines P1 only, and only conditional on a fixed simulator.

Conclusion: discriminating in principle but not reliably discriminating once executed (verdict tracks self-chosen simulator internals), and not executable as a minimal experiment (required timestamped-pool + independent maturation-oracle substrate at AUC-meaningful scale does not exist in this project). Refuted on executability + un-anchored oracle.
